parameters.idea:
We want to build a mobile expense tracker app for small business owners — freelancers, sole traders, and micro-businesses with 1-5 employees. The core problem is that these users currently keep paper receipts in shoeboxes and manually type expenses into spreadsheets at month-end, which is time-consuming, error-prone, and causes them to miss tax deductions.

Users should be able to photograph receipts using their phone camera and the app should automatically extract the amount, date, vendor name, and payment method from the receipt using OCR. It should categorise expenses into standard categories (travel, meals & entertainment, office supplies, software subscriptions, professional services, vehicle/mileage, utilities) and allow users to create custom categories.

The app must generate monthly and quarterly expense reports broken down by category, with totals and trends compared to prior periods. Users need to export reports as PDF and CSV for their accountant. The app should also generate a year-end tax summary matching HMRC self-assessment categories.

Users need to set spending budgets per category per month and receive push notifications when they reach 80% and 100% of their budget. They should also get a weekly spending summary notification every Sunday evening.

The app must work fully offline — users often photograph receipts at client sites or on trains where connectivity is poor. All data should sync automatically when back online, with conflict resolution if the same receipt was edited on multiple devices.

We're building this on AWS (backend) with React Native (mobile app, cross-platform iOS and Android). We need to launch an MVP in 12 weeks. The app must comply with GDPR as we're targeting UK and EU users. We expect to handle up to 10,000 users in the first year with approximately 50 receipts per user per month.

Authentication should use email/password with optional biometric (Face ID / fingerprint) for returning users. We want to integrate with Xero and QuickBooks for automatic expense sync to accounting software in a future phase, but the MVP should work standalone.
parameters.priority_guidance: Core capture and categorisation is most critical for MVP. Reporting and integrations can come later.
